description_long: This object consists of a group of four artificial-looking flowers with long, narrow, lanceolate leaves and petals. The leaves and petals are predominantly green at the base, transitioning to a vibrant red towards the tips, giving a striking two-tone appearance. Each flower has a dense cluster of upright, pointed petals radiating from a central base, with the petals arranged in a radial, whorled fashion. The flowers vary slightly in size but share a similar structure, with no visible reproductive organs or detailed floral anatomy. The stems are short and mostly obscured by the dense foliage at the base. The overall look is ornamental, resembling bromeliads or similar tropical plants, suitable for decorative use in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A group of four ornamental flowers with long, pointed red and green leaves arranged in a dense, radial cluster. The flowers have a vibrant, tropical appearance and are well-suited for decorative urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four flowers with their pointed red and green leaves fanning outward, highlighting the group arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view of a single flower, emphasizing the vertical alignment and density of the leaves and petals.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the backs of the four flowers and the symmetry of their arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view of a single flower, again showing the upright, layered leaves and petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, radial, whorled configuration, radiating symmetrically from the center of each flower. The arrangement is layered, with some petals overlapping others, creating a full, bushy appearance.
